# terrible_dellemc_openmanage_idrac_network (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_config` (String) Allows to enable or disable auto-provisioning to automatically acquire domain name from DHCP.
- `auto_detect` (String) Allows to auto detect the available NIC types used by iDRAC.
- `auto_negotiation` (String) Allows iDRAC to automatically set the duplex mode and network speed.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `dns_from_dhcp` (String) Allows to enable DHCP to obtain DNS server address.
- `dns_idrac_name` (String) Name of the DNS to register iDRAC.
- `duplex_mode` (String) Select the type of data transmission for the NIC.
- `enable_dhcp` (String) Allows to enable or disable Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) in iDRAC.
- `enable_ipv4` (String) Allows to enable or disable IPv4 configuration.
- `enable_nic` (String) Allows to enable or disable the Network Interface Controller (NIC) used by iDRAC.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `failover_network` (String) Select one of the remaining LOMs. If a network fails, the traffic is routed through the failover network.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ip_address` (String) Enter a valid iDRAC static IPv4 address.
- `network_speed` (String) Select the network speed for the selected NIC.
- `nic_mtu` (Number) Maximum Transmission Unit of the NIC.
- `nic_selection` (String) Select one of the available NICs.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `register_idrac_on_dns` (String) Registers iDRAC on a Domain Name System (DNS).
- `setup_idrac_nic_vlan` (String) Allows to configure VLAN on iDRAC.
- `share_mnt` (String) (deprecated)Local mount path of the network share with read-write permission for ansible user. This option is mandatory for network shares. This option is deprecated and will be removed in the later version.
- `share_name` (String) (deprecated)Network share or a local path. This option is deprecated and will be removed in the later version.
- `share_password` (String) (deprecated)Network share user password. This option is mandatory for CIFS share. This option is deprecated and will be removed in the later version.
- `share_user` (String) (deprecated)Network share user name. Use the format 'user@domain' or 'domain\\user' if user is part of a domain. This option is mandatory for CIFS share. This option is deprecated and will be removed in the later version.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `static_dns` (String) Enter the static DNS domain name.
- `static_dns_1` (String) Enter the preferred static DNS server IPv4 address.
- `static_dns_2` (String) Enter the preferred static DNS server IPv4 address.
- `static_gateway` (String) Enter the static IPv4 gateway address to iDRAC.
- `static_net_mask` (String) Enter the static IP subnet mask to iDRAC.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vlan_id` (Number) Enter the VLAN ID.  The VLAN ID must be a number from 1 through 4094.
- `vlan_priority` (Number) Enter the priority for the VLAN ID. The priority value must be a number from 0 through 7.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `error_info` (String) Details of the HTTP Error.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) Successfully configured the idrac network settings.
- `network_status` (String) Status of the Network settings operation job.
